hypothesis: E627 traced E626's margin=6 regression to every required_slot_margin_decode_weight fire landing at frame_depth == 0 (before any component/object frame opens), where a bare visible-slot token is grammar-legal as an alternative to opening a real component and wins the race against the downstream semantic_plan_root* corrective biases that run later in the same per-position bias stack. E627 named two untried mitigations: reorder the bias stack, or exclude frame_depth == 0 from _required_slot_margin_bias's target set. This iteration implements the exclusion and re-runs E626's own matched OOD replay at margin in {0, 2, 6} to check whether margin=2's benefit survives and margin=6's regression is fixed.

mitigation_chosen.which: mitigation (2): exclude frame_depth == 0 from _required_slot_margin_bias's target set
mitigation_chosen.why_not_mitigation_1: Reordering the bias stack (running required_slot_margin_bias after semantic_plan_bias/semantic_plan_root_bias) would still let the floor race the corrective biases at frame_depth==0 -- it would just flip which one applies its delta last, and since both are additive to the same score tensor, the actual final ranking at that position still depends on relative magnitudes, not order (the current order already lets the downstream biases win at margin=2; reordering does not change that margin=6 is large enough to escape any single downstream correction, it only changes which one gets the last word). The exclusion is more principled: it directly encodes the semantic distinction E627 already drew -- this lever's own docstring and E626's design intent were always 'fill still-missing required slots as arguments', never 'choose the root/top-level statement' -- by removing frame_depth==0 from scope entirely, root/top-level choice is left entirely to the semantic_plan_root* family (already responsible for it) with no magnitude race at all, at any margin.
mitigation_chosen.change: Added an optional state parameter; when state is not None and len(state.frames) == 0 (no component/object frame open yet), the bias returns None (does not fire at all) at that position, regardless of margin. The decode loop's call site now passes state=states[row]. Direct unit-test callers that omit state are unaffected (state=None skips the frame_depth check, preserving pre-E628 behavior for those tests).
mitigation_chosen.default_and_gating_unchanged: required_slot_margin_decode_weight stays default 0.0 (off); the E617 slot_contract_constrained_decode contract-gated ValueError guard is untouched.

unit_tests.new: tests/test_models/test_compiler_decode.py::test_required_slot_margin_bias_excludes_frame_depth_zero -- proves the bias is a no-op at frame_depth==0 (state.frames == []) even with a genuinely-missing slot and a nonzero margin that would otherwise floor it (same setup as E626's own floors_only_still_missing_slots test), still fires unchanged at frame_depth==1 (state.frames == [<open component frame>]), and preserves the pre-E628 always-fires behavior when state=None (direct low-level callers).
unit_tests.existing_unchanged: test_required_slot_margin_bias_floors_only_still_missing_slots (E626, no state passed -> unaffected) and test_required_slot_margin_trace_flags_a_root_level_component_hijack (E627, tests _record_required_slot_margin_trace directly with a hand-built margin_bias -- annotated that this exact frame_depth==0 hijack scenario is now unreachable in production via the real bias call site, but the trace function's own labeling logic is still validly covered).

checkpoint_reused.note: Reused E626's own already-trained scratch checkpoint verbatim (no retrain) -- exact same file, sha256 verified to match E626's JSON before running.

eval_recipe.note: Identical eval recipe to E626/E627's matched replay (same fixed E617-era flags), same checkpoint, same suite/n. The only variable across this run and E626's is the code under test (E628's frame_depth==0 exclusion).

treatment_margin6.dashboard_regression_fixed: predicted root = Stack([v0, v1, v3, v4], ...) with Callout + Cards, not the bare Button E626 reported at margin=6
treatment_margin6.gallery_regression_fixed: ImageGallery array is [{src: ':ood.gallery.img', alt: ':ood.gallery.alt'}], not the empty array E626 reported at margin=6

margin2_vs_control.vs_e626_margin2: identical to E626's own margin=2 arm to 4 decimal places on every headline metric -- margin=2's benefit fully survives the fix, as expected (E627 already showed margin=2 never hit the frame_depth==0 failure mode).

margin6_vs_control.vs_e626_margin6: E626 measured margin=6 well BELOW control on every headline metric (fidelity 0.30, validity 0.48, reward 0.5693); post-fix margin=6 is now IDENTICAL to margin=2 and strictly above control on every metric except ast_edge_f1 (same small dip already present at margin=2 in E626). The regression is fixed, not merely reduced, in this n=4 replay.

comparison.margin2_vs_margin6_post_fix: byte-identical per-record predictions across all 4 OOD records; identical required_slot_margin_applications_sum (17) and choice_changes_sum (9); identical frame_depth distribution (1, 2, 3 only, zero fires at frame_depth 0). At this dosage range the fix removes the dose-dependence entirely observed in this replay -- margin no longer matters between 2 and 6 because neither ever competes for root/top-level choice anymore.

decision: The frame_depth==0 exclusion (mitigation 2) is a real, verified fix, not merely a plausible one: margin=6 -- previously the worst arm in E626, regressing every headline metric below control -- now produces predictions byte-identical to margin=2's already-verified-positive arm, on the same checkpoint, same records, same eval recipe. Dashboard's collapse-to-bare-Button and Gallery's empty-array regressions (E626's two headline failure signatures) are both independently confirmed fixed by inspecting the actual predicted programs, not just the aggregate metrics. Decode-stats traces confirm the mechanism directly: required_slot_margin fires 17 times in both the margin=2 and margin=6 arms, all at frame_depth in {1, 2, 3}, zero at frame_depth 0 -- exactly the exclusion working as designed. Margin=2's benefit is unaffected (identical to E626's own margin=2 numbers to 4 decimal places). This raises required_slot_margin_decode_weight's practical safety: at least across this n=4 replay, dosage within {2, 6} is no longer a live risk once the fix lands. This remains a single small (n=4) matched-pair OOD replay on one scratch checkpoint, not a confirmatory multi-seed/full-suite result -- not a ship claim. No checkpoint trained, promoted, or synced. binding_aware_meaningful_v2_rate_strict stays 0.0 in every arm, unchanged from E626 -- this fix targets structural hijacking, not strict-v2 placeholder/role failures.
next_step: 1) E626's original next-step -- a powered multi-seed replay sweeping required_slot_margin_decode_weight across a small grid on a full held-out/rico_held suite -- remains open and untouched by this iteration; now safe to include margin values above 2 in that grid given this fix, though still only demonstrated at n=4 on one checkpoint here. 2) This n=4 replay happened to make margin=2 and margin=6 produce identical predictions; a wider margin sweep (e.g. 10, 20) or a different checkpoint/seed could still reveal a *different* failure mode once the floor becomes large enough to win the local frame_depth>=1 argument-position competition against other legitimate slot-filling or closure biases -- that has not been tested here and should not be assumed safe at arbitrary margin. 3) binding_aware_meaningful_v2_rate_strict remains 0.0 at every margin tested; the coverage-aware component/property closure work E620 originally named is still the next lever after this one, not another margin/scope tuning pass.

version_stamp.note: code_commit/code_dirty are the code_git_sha reported by the eval runs before this iteration's commit lands; model.twotower v61 reflects the version bump made in this same change.
